ground_truth.title: Home Remodeling Costs in Los Angeles (PPSF & More!)
ground_truth.author: Sweeten
ground_truth.main_content:
The City of Angels offers a diverse range of architectural styles, from charming bungalows to sleek modern masterpieces. However, the cost of bringing your renovation vision to life can vary significantly. Our guide to home remodeling costs in Los Angeles includes kitchen, bath, and whole-home (plus ADUs and permit tips!)

Summary of home remodeling costs in Los Angeles:
- Full home renovation: $125-$500+ per square foot (psf)
- Budget kitchen: Starting at $28,000 (based on a 200-sq-ft kitchen)
- Mid-grade kitchen: Starting at $45,000
- High-end kitchen: Starting at $85,000
- Budget bathroom: Starting at $22,000 (based on a 35-sq-ft space)
- Mid-grade bathroom: Starting at $32,000 (based on a 35-sq-ft space)
- High-end bathroom: Starting at $45,000
- Mid-grade master suite addition: $550 psf or $220,000 (based on a 24' x 16' space)
- High-end master suite addition: $650 psf or $416,000 (based on a 32' x 20' space)
- Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU): Starting at $60,000 for a conversion and $150,000+ for ground-up construction.

Moving into a new house—or realizing your home hasn't kept up with your lifestyle—can make renovation feel like a path to reinvention. Creating a budget for that planned remodel is unique to every project. The materials you choose, changes in layout, or moving plumbing or gas lines, all factor into the equation.

For a full-house renovation, average costs range from $125 to $500+ per square foot. Dry rooms, like living rooms and bedrooms, tend to be less expensive to renovate than wet rooms, such as kitchens and bathrooms. While dry rooms might fall on the lower end of this range, wet rooms could start closer to $400 per square foot.

When preparing for a kitchen remodel, remember that California banned the running of new gas lines to any new construction—which means no gas stoves. Budget kitchen remodeling costs start at $24,500 for 200 square feet with budget-friendly finishes. Mid-grade kitchen remodeling costs come in at $37,500 including new stock or semi-custom cabinets. High-end kitchen projects start at $76,000 with custom cabinetry.

For bathroom remodels, budget bathroom renovation at 35 square feet starts at $18,000-$25,000. Mid-grade bathroom costs average between $28,500-$29,744 ($849 psf). High-end bathroom renovation and expansion from 35 to 100 square feet costs around $88,491.

Master suite additions: Mid-grade (24' x 16') costs approximately $503 psf or $193,390. Upscale master suite (32' x 20') costs approximately $606 psf or $388,427.

ADU costs in Los Angeles range from $50,000 to over $200,000. Converting an existing structure averages around $75,000, while ground-up constructions start between $125,000 and $150,000. Garage conversions can cost as little as $58,000 up to $196,000.

Permit costs in Los Angeles range from $750 to $2,500. Express permits for kitchen and bath remodels cost around $500 to $1,000. Bigger projects start at $5,000 and can go up to $20,000 in cities like Santa Monica.

Interior designer fees are typically calculated as a flat fee for renovation projects, offering transparency between designer and client.
